stem: An engineer takes 10 measurements of room temperature using a thermometer. The measurements have a mean of 40.6 °C and a standard deviation of 6.1 °C. However, calibration tests reveal that a thermometer systematically overestimates the true value by between 2.2% and 8.1%. Estimate the true room temperature and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 40.6 °C. After correcting for the 5.2% bias: ≈ 38.6 °C. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.2%-8.1%) and sampling variability: [33.7, 43.6]. Point estimate: 38.6 °C.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
